page: Amazon Web Services r7gd.8xlarge is a memory-optimized server powered by custom AWS Graviton3 processors utilizing the 64-bit ARMv8 Neoverse-V1 architecture. It features 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs running at 2.6 GHz, supported by 64 KB L1D cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core.

This server includes 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage, delivering high-speed local read and write capabilities directly attached via the Nitro hypervisor. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 15 Gbps. The hardware configuration lacks dedicated GPU accelerators, relying entirely on the ARM64 CPU for computational tasks.

In benchmark testing, the r7gd.8xlarge demonstrates top-tier memory performance, with its PassMark memory score ranking in the top 10%. Large-block uncached memory read and write bandwidths reach 235.99 GB/sec and 105.26 GB/sec respectively, both placing in the top 25%. Multi-core processing is also strong, as evidenced by a Geekbench multi-core score of 17567.0 and high throughput in PassMark database operations. However, the ARM64 architecture shows limitations in large-scale LLM inference, where prompt processing for 70B models falls into the bottom 10% of tested systems.

The custom Graviton processor design provides high resource density and qualitative cost-efficiency for memory-bound workloads compared to traditional x86 alternatives. Given the combination of 256 GB RAM, fast local NVMe storage, and strong multi-core memory bandwidth, this server is highly suited for memory-intensive databases, caching layers, real-time data analytics, and high-throughput web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services r7gd.8xlarge is a memory-optimized, ARM64-based server powered by a 32-core AWS Graviton processor running at 2.6 GHz. It features 256.0 GB of RAM, providing a high 8.0 GB per core ratio, alongside 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and 15 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers top-tier memory performance, with PassMark memory scores in the top 10% and strong uncached large-block memory read speeds of 235.99 GB/sec. While multi-core CPU performance and database operations are highly competitive, the server exhibits lower efficiency in heavy LLM text generation tasks. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive applications, making it an optimal fit for high-performance databases, caching layers, and real-time data analytics.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7gd.8xlarge is a memory-optimized ARM64 server featuring 32 Graviton cores, 256 GB of RAM, and 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier memory performance for database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r7gd.8xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized server with 32 cores, 256 GB RAM, and 1900 GB local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: An ARM64 memory-optimized instance featuring high-speed local NVMe storage and top-tier memory bandwidth for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated AWS Graviton ARM64 cores running at 2.6 GHz
256 GB of system memory with top-tier read/write bandwidth
1900 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD instance storage
15 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Optimal for memory-intensive databases, caching, and analytics
